  ← packages-rex:token-usage-analytics: packages/rex/src/cli/commands/usage.ts → packages/rex/src/store/index.ts; packages/rex/src/cli/commands/usage.ts → packages/rex/src/store/index.ts; packages/rex/src/core/token-usage.ts → packages/rex/src/store/index.ts; packages/rex/src/core/token-usage.ts → packages/rex/src/store/index.ts

</imports>

<insights>

- 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- The healthy cohesion/coupling balance (0.6/0.4) confirms this zone is well-bounded — storage concerns are cleanly separated from domain logic.
- Integration schemas for Jira and Notion are co-located with the adapter registry, which is correct: schema definitions belong next to the adapters that consume them.
- canonical.ts as a utility in this zone is a good fit — canonical serialization is fundamentally a storage-layer concern ensuring deterministic JSON round-trips.
- Highest cohesion score in this batch (0.6) — this zone demonstrates well-scoped responsibility and is a model for how the larger zones could be organized.
- 5 imports from store→core and 10 from core→store indicate bidirectional coupling at the domain/storage boundary; verify this is intentional layering rather than a circular dependency.
- Zone "prd-storage-integrations" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- prd-storage-integrations has zero coupling to prd-fix-command and task-selection zones — confirming storage is correctly isolated from all feature-specific domain logic in this batch; it only couples to cli, core, and unit-analyze as expected for a persistence layer.
- Storage zone has no cross-zone edges to any feature command zone (fix, task-selection, usage) — a clean separation that prevents feature churn from destabilizing storage contracts.
- The store zone's bidirectional coupling with core (store→core 5 imports, core→store 10 imports) means the domain core directly manages persistence details rather than depending on an abstract repository interface. This is the same SCC risk noted globally but manifests concretely here as store calling into core and core calling back into store — neither layer has a clean abstraction surface.
- integration-schemas/ is the only subdirectory-namespaced sub-module in this batch — its index.ts acts as a mini-public-API for its schema definitions. This sub-module nesting pattern (named subdirectory + index.ts) is the correct internal organization strategy for complex zones, but is not applied to prd-core-operations despite that zone having at least three naturally grouped subsets (analytics/coverage, sync/canonical, health/keywords). The pattern exists as a working example one zone away from where it is most needed.
- The adapter-registry pattern is unique in the package — no other zone implements a plugin registry. Its correctness guarantees are currently enforced only by convention: an adapter that partially implements contracts.ts will be accepted at registration time and fail at runtime. As new adapters (beyond Jira/Notion) are added, this deferred validation becomes an increasing source of hard-to-diagnose runtime errors.
- Apply the integration-schemas/ sub-module pattern (named subdirectory + index.ts barrel) to prd-core-operations by grouping analytics.ts/code-coverage.ts, sync-engine.ts/sync.ts/canonical.ts, and health.ts/keywords.ts into named subdirectories. This imposes internal structure without requiring a zone split and mirrors the only working example of this pattern in the batch.
- adapter-registry.ts should validate that a registered adapter fully implements all contracts.ts interface methods at registration time rather than deferring failures to runtime use. Fail-fast registration converts a hard-to-diagnose runtime error into an immediate, actionable error at adapter setup.
- [call graph] 231 internal calls, 48 outgoing, 53 incoming (cohesion: 0.83, coupling: 0.17)

</insights>
